message:
fix(dataflow): fire sink callbacks only when node value actually changes

Sink callbacks were previously fired for every sink node that was
reachable during propagation, regardless of whether the node's value
actually changed. This caused unnecessary callback invocations when
upstream sources changed but the downstream computation produced the
same value (e.g. a threshold function that remains true for different
input magnitudes).

Now tracks which nodes had their values mutated during propagation in
a changed_nodes HashSet, and only fires sink callbacks for nodes in
that set rather than the broader reachable set.

Added test sink_callback_not_fired_when_value_unchanged which verifies:
- A threshold map (>0 → true) does NOT fire when source changes 1→2
  (output stays true)
- It DOES fire exactly once when source changes to 0 (output flips
  to false)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
